flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

1.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: broadness. i.e. (concrete) the ear (from its form in man)
strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity)
{something said}
to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away (including moral deflection); used in a great variety of application

tokens.text: upon thee for thou wilt hear
tokens.range: properly, to eye or (generally) to heed, i.e. pay attention; by implication, to respond; by extension to begin to speak; specifically to sing, shout, testify, announce
to call out to (i.e. properly, address by name, but used in a wide variety of applications)
to hear intelligently (often with implication of attention, obedience, etc.; causatively, to tell, etc.)
hating; an adversary
this or that
properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)
to strike with more or less violence (beat, fell, corrode); by implication (of attack) to knock together, i.e. surround or circulate
the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)
morally wrong; concretely, an (actively) bad person
properly, to be burly, i.e. (figuratively) powerful (passively, impregnable); by implication, to ravage

flags.note: נֶפֶשׁ — "soul" (Greek-influenced: non-material self) vs. "life" (the whole living person) vs. "self" (the person as subject). Hebrew nefesh is embodied, not a separate immaterial substance.

flags.note: חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.
נֶפֶשׁ — "soul" (Greek-influenced: non-material self) vs. "life" (the whole living person) vs. "self" (the person as subject). Hebrew nefesh is embodied, not a separate immaterial substance.
